author: Robert Carr
description: A cold wheat bread sandwich with cheddar, a chopped pickle and greens crunch, onion, and a quick mayo-Dijon-ketchup sauce.
ingredients: 1 to 2 slices sharp or mild cheddar cheese
Small handful lettuce or baby arugula, patted dry and thinly sliced
4 to 5 dill pickle slices, patted dry and chopped
A few very thin onion slices, optional
1/2 teaspoon pickle brine from the jar
instructions: Stir the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, ketchup, and pickle brine together in a small bowl.
Put the chopped pickles, sliced lettuce or arugula, and onion slices on a cutting board or plate.
Add 1 teaspoon of the sauce to the pickle mixture and toss with your fingers until lightly coated.
Spread the remaining sauce on one side of each slice of wheat bread.
Place cheddar on both sauced bread slices if using 2 cheese slices, or place 1 cheese slice on the bottom bread if using 1 slice.
Pile the pickle and greens crunch over the cheddar.
Close the sandwich, press it gently with your hand, and cut it in half.
notes: This sandwich is meant for a fast no-cook lunch. For a warmer version, toast it in a skillet or make a grilled cheese.
Patting the pickles and greens dry keeps the bread from getting soggy.
Use mild cheddar for a softer flavor or sharp cheddar when you want the sandwich to taste more savory.
This also works with a few slices of salami or prosciutto if you want a meatier sandwich.
Toast the bread first if you want more crunch.
title: Pickle Cheddar Sandwich
